statement.markdown: Write a function to validate Docker image tags. A valid image tag should be in the format of '<image-name>:<tag>' or '<image-name>'. The function should return True if the tag is valid, and False otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the Docker image tag.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the tag is valid.
statement.notes: The function should handle both valid and invalid tags. No tags will contain any spaces or special characters.

hints.text: The problem statement does not require any complex algorithms. Just a simple string parsing will do.

editorial.title: Docker Image Tag Validation
editorial.intuition: To solve this problem, we can simply use string manipulation functions to check if the input tag matches the required format.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible approach is to check each character in the input string to ensure it matches the required format.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a regular expression to check if the input tag matches the required format.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is that the solution will return True for all valid tags and False for all invalid tags.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Check if the input tag starts with a '<' and ends with a '>'. Then check if the tag contains a ':' and split it into two parts. If the tag contains a '>', return False.
commonMistakes: Not handling the case where the tag ends with a '>'
Not handling the case where the tag contains a ':'

starterCode.javascript: // Function to validate Docker image tags
starterCode.python: // Function to validate Docker image tags

buggyStarterCode.javascript: function validateTag(tag) { if (tag.startsWith('<') && tag.endsWith('>')) { return true; } return false; }

solutions.javascript:
// Solution
function validateTag(tag) { if (tag.startsWith('<') && tag.endsWith('>')) { var parts = tag.split(':'); if (parts.length > 2) return false; return true; } return false; }
